2603.28217.title: An Optimal Battery-Free Approach for Emission Reduction by Storing Solar Surplus in Building Thermal Mass
2603.28217.paper_summary: Decarbonization in buildings calls for advanced control strategies that coordinate on-site renewables, grid electricity, and thermal demand. Literature approaches typically rely on demand side management strategies or on active energy storage, like batteries. However, the first solution often neglects carbon-aware objectives, and could lead to grid overload issues, while batteries entail environmental, end-of-life, and cost concerns. To overcome these limitations, we propose an optimal, carbon-aware optimization strategy that exploits the building's thermal mass as a passive storage, avoiding dedicated batteries. Specifically, when a surplus of renewable energy is available, our strategy computes the optimal share of surplus to store by temporarily adjusting the indoor temperature setpoint within comfort bounds. Thus, by explicitly accounting for forecasts of building energy consumption, solar production, and time-varying grid carbon intensity, our strategy enables emissions-aware load shifting while maintaining comfort. We evaluate the approach by simulating three TRNSYS models of the same system with different thermal mass. In all cases, the results show consistent reductions in grid electricity consumption with respect to a baseline that does not leverage surplus renewable generation. These findings highlight the potential of thermal-mass-based control for building decarbonization.
2603.28217.paper_summary_zh: 建築物的脫碳需要先進的控制策略，以協調現場可再生能源、電網電力和熱需求。文獻中的方法通常依賴於需求側管理策略或主動能源儲存，例如電池。然而，第一種解決方案往往忽略了碳意識目標，並可能導致電網過載問題，而電池則涉及環境、使用壽命和成本等問題。為了克服這些限制，我們提出了一種最佳的碳意識優化策略，利用建築的熱質量作為被動儲存，避免使用專用電池。具體而言，當可再生能源過剩時，我們的策略通過在舒適範圍內臨時調整室內溫度設定點來計算最佳的過剩儲存比例。因此，通過明確考慮建築能耗預測、太陽能產量和隨時間變化的電網碳強度，我們的策略實現了意識到排放的負載轉移，同時保持舒適。我們通過模擬三個不同熱質量的相同系統的TRNSYS模型來評估這種方法。在所有情況下，結果顯示與不利用過剩可再生發電的基準相比，電網電力消耗持續減少。這些發現突顯了基於熱質量控制在建築脫碳中的潛力。
2603.28217.author: Michela Boffi et.al.
2603.28217.authors: Michela Boffi, Jessica Leoni, Fabrizio Leonforte, Mara Tanelli, Paolo Oliaro
